source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/4. A1417MCW/6. IEC Test Report (EMC, Safety)/PRJ0042257 REP028549 CBTR IEC60601-1 H&abyz A1717MCW (Original)_CSV.pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report No. 269183 with National Deviations and Attachments
document.documentType: CB Test Report / IEC 60601-1 safety test report excerpt

sourceSummary.oneLine: Excerpt from IEC 60601-1 safety testing for Report No. 269183, covering thermal, single-fault, transformer, national deviation, and attached adapter CB certificate information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory evidence for IEC 60601-1 basic safety compliance, national deviations, and component/adapter certificate traceability.
keyPoints: DEPS9601 excessive temperature testing passed at 90 V/60 Hz and 264 V/50 Hz with measured values corrected to 40 °C.
Electrical single fault and cooling impairment tests were marked pass with no hazardous situation reported.
Several mechanical, ingress, pressure vessel, X-radiation, ME system leakage, and handling tables were marked N/A.
National deviations for Canada, Japan, Korea, Switzerland, and the United States are included as Attachment 2.
Attachment 6 includes IECEE CB certificates for BRIDGEPOWER AC/DC adaptor models and related report E302267-D1020-1/A1/C1-CB.

entities.name: DEPS9601
entities.evidence: Pages 127-128 list Model No.: DEPS9601 for excessive temperature testing.

entities.name: Report No. 269183
entities.evidence: Each main report page in this excerpt is headed Report No. 269183.

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Main report pages are headed IEC 60601-1 and TRF No. IEC60601_1J.

entities.name: BRIDGEPOWER CORP
entities.evidence: CB certificates identify BRIDGEPOWER CORP as applicant, manufacturer, and factory.

entities.name: AC/DC Adaptor
entities.evidence: Attachment 6 CB certificates list Product: AC/DC Adaptor.

entities.name: UL International Demko A/S
entities.evidence: Adapter CB report page 1 lists CB Testing Laboratory: UL International Demko A/S, Borupvang 5A, 2750 Ballerup, Denmark.

entities.name: T1 switching transformer
entities.evidence: Attachment 4 is titled Specification of switching transformer, T1.

entities.name: DK-51428-A1-M1-UL
entities.evidence: Attachment 6 includes CB Test Certificate Ref. Certif. No. DK-51428-A1-M1-UL.

entities.name: DK-61570-M1-UL
entities.evidence: Attachment 6 includes CB Test Certificate Ref. Certif. No. DK-61570-M1-UL.

concepts.name: Excessive temperature testing
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.1.1 tables for DEPS9601 are marked P with thermocouple measurements at 90 V/60 Hz and 264 V/50 Hz.

concepts.name: Electrical single fault conditions
concepts.evidence: Clause 13.2 tables are marked P and list short-circuit, overload, and cooling impairment results with no hazardous situation.

concepts.name: Transformer overload and short-circuit testing
concepts.evidence: Clauses 15.5.1.2 and 15.5.1.3 are marked P and refer to appended table 13.2 results for switching transformer T1.

concepts.name: National deviations
concepts.evidence: Attachment 2 contains Canadian, Japan, Korea, Switzerland, and US national difference sections.

concepts.name: CB Scheme adapter certification
concepts.evidence: Attachment 6 includes IECEE CB Test Certificates for AC/DC Adaptor models and certificate references.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601 passed IEC 60601-1 excessive temperature testing at 90 V / 60 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 127: Clause 11.1.1 table marked P; Model No. DEPS9601; ambient 23.8 °C; supply 90 V / 60 Hz; measured temperatures corrected to 40 °C.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601 passed IEC 60601-1 excessive temperature testing at 264 V / 50 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 128: Clause 11.1.1 table marked P; Model No. DEPS9601; ambient 23.9 °C; supply 264 V / 50 Hz; measured temperatures corrected to 40 °C.

evidence.claim: X-radiation measurement was not applicable, with a maximum allowable radiation reference of 36 pA/kg, 5 μSv/h, 0.5 mR/h.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 126: Clause 10.1.1 Measurement of X-radiation marked N/A and lists maximum allowable radiation 36 pA/kg (5 μSv/h) (0.5 mR/h).

evidence.claim: Single fault short-circuit of +16 Vdc output to accessible metal enclosure produced immediate output shutdown and no hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 131: Short circuit +16 Vdc output to accessible metal enclosure; input 264 V, 0.07 A, 10 min; all output shut down immediately; no hazard; hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: Multiple Clause 13.2.2 electrical single fault short-circuit tests were assessed as no hazardous situation.
evidence.evidenceText: Pages 132-133 list short circuits of C13, PC2, PC1, C8, C5, U4, U1, and T1 with hazardous situation marked NO.

evidence.claim: C5 short-circuit caused F1 and F2 to open and U1 damage, but the result was still no hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 133: Short circuit C5; unit shut down immediately; F1, F2 opened, U1 component damaged; no hazard; hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: The +16 Vdc output overload test stabilized transformer T1 winding temperature at 83.1 °C with no hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 133: Overload +16 Vdc o/p; input 264 V; test time 4 h 20 min; DC output overload 3.60 A; T1 winding temperature stabilized at 83.1 °C; ambient 23.6 °C; no hazard.

evidence.claim: Cooling impairment with all openings blocked at 90 V resulted in normal operation and no hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 134: All openings blocked; input voltage 90 V; input current 1.07 A; test time 1 h 4 min; normal operated; transformer T1 coil 94.5 °C; ambient 23.4 °C; no hazard.

evidence.claim: Mechanical strength tests were not applicable and the equipment was described as a power supply unit to be evaluated in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 136: Clause 15.3 Mechanical Strength tests marked N/A; supplementary information states the equipment is a power supply unit and to be additionally evaluated in the end product.

evidence.claim: Transformer short-circuit and overload test tables were marked pass and referenced switching transformer T1 results in appended table 13.2.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 137: Clauses 15.5.1.2 and 15.5.1.3 marked P; supplementary information states see appended table 13.2 results for switching transformers, T1.

evidence.claim: Canada national deviations were assessed against CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 1: CA - Canadian National Differences to CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08; differences according to Canadian National standard CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08.

evidence.claim: Japan national differences were assessed against JIS T0601-1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 6: Japan National Differences; differences according to JIS T0601-1:2012 (IEC 60601-1:2005 Ed. 3 MOD).

evidence.claim: Korea national differences accepted equipment rated 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz for supply voltage and frequency limitations.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 19: Korea National Differences; supply voltages 110, 220 V and 380 V, equipment rated 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, equipment rating includes requirement, verdict P.

evidence.claim: US national differences were assessed against ANSI/AAMI ES60601-1:2005 / A2:2010.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 21: US National Differences; differences according to US National standard ANSI/AAMI ES60601-1: 2005 / A2:2010.

evidence.claim: Attachment 6 includes CB certificate DK-51428-A1-M1-UL for a BRIDGEPOWER AC/DC Adaptor issued 2017-03-08.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 6 certificate lists Ref. Certif. No. DK-51428-A1-M1-UL, Product AC/DC Adaptor, BRIDGEPOWER CORP, Date 2017-03-08, Original Issue Date 2016-01-22.

evidence.claim: Attachment 6 includes CB certificate DK-61570-M1-UL for a BRIDGEPOWER AC/DC Adaptor issued 2017-03-08.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 6 certificate lists Ref. Certif. No. DK-61570-M1-UL, Product AC/DC Adaptor, BRIDGEPOWER CORP, Date 2017-03-08, Original Issue Date 2017-02-21.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 10 of 13, so document-level title, product model scope, and final conclusions may be incomplete outside this excerpt.
Attachment 4 switching transformer specification contains substantial OCR corruption; only clearly legible facts should be relied upon.
Attachment 6 concerns AC/DC adaptor CB certificates and report E302267-D1020-1/A1/C1-CB, which appear to be supporting attachments rather than the main Report No. 269183 test object.
